sections.paragraph:
μετενδύω (Lucian et al.) mid. (since Strabo, also Joseph.) change clothes, aor. pct. μετενδυσαμένη σκυθρωπότερα ἱμάτια ἀπῆλθεν πρὸς αὐτόν she (Armetilla) changed into darker clothes and came to him (Paul) AcPl Ha 2, 16.—DELG s.v. δύω.

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 642
